action.content:
You are a field linguist. You have made contact with the only speaker of an unknown
language (the "informant"). Nobody has ever written down this language before. Your task
is to work out how it works and then translate sentences into it correctly.

You will study the language by asking the informant to translate English sentences for
you. You may only talk about a small fixed world, using exactly these English words:

Nouns: farmer, ship, stone, hill, dog, boat, lake, road, river, hunter, spear, bird, tree, king
Adjectives: old, dark, tall, big, blue, small, cold, red
Verbs (write them in this base form): see, watch, hold, carry, find, hear, chase, feed
Articles: the (definite), a (indefinite)
Number words: two, three, four, five, six (no number word = one / singular)
These are ALL the words in play - the language has no other words.

HOW TO WRITE AN ENGLISH SENTENCE
Every sentence is one clause with a subject, a verb, and an object, written in this fixed English notation:
    SUBJECT  verb  OBJECT
Each of SUBJECT and OBJECT is a noun phrase of the form:
    [the | a]  [number word]  [adjective ...]  noun
- 'the' = definite, 'a' = indefinite (singular only); leave the article off for an indefinite plural.
- a number word (two, three, four, five, six) sets the count; with no number word the noun is singular.
- zero or more adjectives may come before the noun.
Examples of well-formed English sentences you may send:
    a farmer see a ship
    six farmers see a ship
    a old farmer see a ship

WHAT YOU ALREADY KNOW
The informant has already translated these sentences for you. Each line is
"<English>  =>  <the language>":
    the two big roads hear the dog  =>  tuko fidutofato kagugu teki bobato
    the six hunters carry four red hills  =>  befe tafipafato pobo ba gigofa bade
    four hills feed the five ships  =>  ba gigofa bogago bi fadafato
    the five red farmers chase a small tree  =>  bi botafato bade pofo toki gidido
    the four trees watch the old king  =>  ba tokifato gufaki kagito bugu
    the river watch the two dark birds  =>  figaketo gufaki tuko dufifato defe
    the five red trees watch the two cold trees  =>  bi tokifato bade gufaki tuko tokifato gida
    the two small hunters hear the three lakes  =>  tuko tafipafato gidido teki guba gagofato
    six old stones feed the six rivers  =>  befe bukadafa bugu bogago befe figakefato
    a blue road watch a cold hunter  =>  fiduto kabage gufaki tafipa gida
    the cold ship find a small boat  =>  fadato gida piki degogo gidido
    five big boats see a tall spear  =>  bi degogofa kagugu peke kuko fubu
    six small hunters hold a tree  =>  befe tafipafa gidido kipi toki

HOW THE SESSION WORKS
1. INVESTIGATION: You have 5 rounds of questioning. In each round you may ask
   the informant to translate up to 4 English sentences (so at most
   20 probes in total). The informant answers each one truthfully and
   exactly. Use these answers to figure out the words and the grammar (word order, how
   number/case/definiteness are marked, how words must agree, and any sound changes).
   Choose your probes deliberately - test specific hypotheses.
2. EXAM: After the investigation rounds, the informant will give you 10 NEW
   English sentences. You must translate each one INTO the language. Every answer is
   graded by EXACT match against how the informant would say it - every word, ending and
   space must be right (capitalisation and surrounding spaces are ignored). The exam
   sentences will use the same vocabulary but in combinations you have not been shown, so
   you must induce the general rules, not just memorise examples.

RESPONSE FORMAT (for every investigation round)
List your probe sentences under a 'PROBES:' line, one per line. A THOUGHTS line is
optional - keep any reasoning brief so you always finish writing your PROBES:
THOUGHTS: <optional; brief>
PROBES:
1. <an English sentence, written as described above>
2. <another English sentence>
(up to 4 lines; at least one)

Begin your first round of probes now.
action.label: context

action.content:
PROBES:
1. a blue boat see a red bird
2. the two cold trees watch the one big river
3. a small boat feed the one big river
4. the one big river see the one big bird
5. the one big bird see the one big tree

TRANSLATIONS:

2. "the two cold trees watch the big river"  =>  tuko tokifato gida gufaki figaketo kagugu
3. "a small boat feed the big river"  =>  degogo gidido bogago figaketo kagugu
4. "the big river see the big bird"  =>  figaketo kagugu peke dufito kagugu

action.content:
TRANSLATIONS:
1. "the big bird see the big tree"  =>  dufito kagugu peke tokito kagugu
2. "the two big birds see the big tree"  =>  tuko dufifato kagugu peke tokito kagugu
3. "the big bird see the two big trees"  =>  dufito kagugu peke tuko tokifato kagugu
4. "the two big birds see the two big trees"  =>  tuko dufifato kagugu peke tuko tokifato kagugu

action.content:
TRANSLATIONS:
1. "the big bird see the big stone"  =>  dufito kagugu peke bukadato kagugu
2. "the two big birds see the big stone"  =>  tuko dufifato kagugu peke bukadato kagugu
3. "the big bird see the two big stones"  =>  dufito kagugu peke tuko bukadafato kagugu
4. "the two big birds see the two big stones"  =>  tuko dufifato kagugu peke tuko bukadafato kagugu
STATUS: investigation round 4 of 5 done. Probes used: 16 of 20. 1 investigation round(s) left before the exam (10 sentences).
Send your next probes now (a PROBES list; brief THOUGHTS optional).

action.content:

2. the two big birds see the big road

the two big birds see the big road
the big bird see the two big roads

action.content:
TRANSLATIONS:
1. "the big bird see the big road"  =>  dufito kagugu peke fidutoto kagugu
2. "the two big birds see the big road"  =>  tuko dufifato kagugu peke fidutoto kagugu
3. "the big bird see the two big roads"  =>  dufito kagugu peke tuko fidutofato kagugu
4. "the two big birds see the two big roads"  =>  tuko dufifato kagugu peke tuko fidutofato kagugu

FINAL EXAM. Investigation is over. Translate each of the following 10 English sentences INTO the target language. Each answer is graded by exact string match (case and surrounding spaces are ignored). Every sentence uses only words you have already seen translated.

1. two tall rivers chase two big dogs
2. five cold hills chase five small kings
3. the four red roads hold six kings
4. three lakes carry the six hunters
5. a tree hold the tall river
6. the two red hills watch the four cold dogs
7. the four tall birds feed the two ships
8. the farmer hold the five dogs
9. four birds see two spears
10. the two lakes watch three small spears

Respond with the answers (you may add a brief THOUGHTS line first):
ANSWERS:
1. <translation of sentence 1>
... through 10.
action.label: context
